arts.ttl: Juventus and Porto seek loan move for Carney Chukwuemeka but Blues would rather sell former Aston Villa midfielder for £40m release clause
arts.sum: Juventus and Porto are the latest clubs to enquire about a loan deal for Chelsea's Carney Chukwuemeka. Chelsea have been trying to steer clubs towards his £40m buy-out clause

arts.ttl: Man City reject James McAtee loan offer as Bayer Leverkusen turn focus to Aston Villa attacker?
arts.sum: Manchester City reportedly reject a loan proposal for James McAtee as Bayer Leverkusen switch their focus to an Aston Villa attacker.

arts.ttl: Battle of Britain clash will provide McGinn with chance to show Celtic just what they missed out on
arts.sum: As widely welcomed as UEFA's new-look Champions League has been, the one enduring criticism of it is a lack of jeopardy.

arts.ttl: Celtic complete Jota return as Kyogo joins Rennes
arts.sum: Celtic secure the return of Portuguese winger Jota from Rennes as Kyogo Furuhashi joins the French club.

arts.ttl: What now for Celtic and returning Jota after Kyogo exit?
arts.sum: Kyogo Furuhashi has left Celtic for Rennes, but who will replace the striker up front as Jota returns to the Scottish champions from the French club?

arts.ttl: New Celtic signing Jota on claims Saudi riches have dulled his sporting ambitions
arts.sum: New Celtic signing Jota has addressed suggestions the riches he banked during his time in Saudi Arabia may have dulled his sporting ambitions.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa enquire over out-of-favour Chelsea star Joao Felix... with former Atletico Madrid man starting just THREE times in the Premier League since his £46m move last summer
arts.sum: Felix, 25, joined Chelsea for a reported £46million from Atletico Madrid last summer but has struggled for sufficient game time under Enzo Maresca since signing his six-year deal at Stamford Bridge.

arts.ttl: Jota back at Celtic for silver NOT gold as one of the 'last romantics' shuts down hunger myth
arts.sum: Jota is back at Celtic and he's hungrier than ever

arts.ttl: Evan Ferguson to Celtic transfer may already have Brighton's answer as blockbuster loan hinges on 3 things
arts.sum: The Hoops are chasing a new striker and could Ferguson be their man?

Fixtures Aston Villa U21 Vs. Fulham U21 - Mon, Jan 27th 2025, 19:00 Aston Villa suffered late heartbreak as Fulham grabbed a last-gasp injury-time leveller to snatch a 3-3 draw in Premier League 2. The Young Lions looked to be heading for all three points against the high-flying Cottagers at Bodymoor Heath until Bradley De Jesus equalised with almost the last kick of the game in the 92nd minute. Jamaldeen Jimoh-Aloba had earlier bagged a first-half brace and went agonisingly close to completing his hat-trick moments before Fulham had the final say. The forward opening the scoring with a delightful piece of skill and finish after eight minutes, before doubling his tally midway through the first half as the Young Lions pounced on a defensive error. But Fulham, second in the PL2 table having won 10 of their 12 matches heading into the encounter, levelled courtesy of two goals in three minutes from Aaron Loupalo-Bi and Terrell Works. Brad Burrowes immediately fired Villa back in front in the 38th minute, and that was how it remained until the six-goal thriller was wrapped up in added time, with Josep Gombau’s side cruelly denied a long-awaited league victory. The Young Lions went into the encounter on the back of a 5-0 triumph over Sutton Coldfield Town to reach the quarter-finals of the Birmingham Senior Cup, although they were winless in seven PL2 matches. It was a bright start from the hosts as Kyrie Pierre tested Fulham ‘keeper Marco Underwood before Jimoh-Aloba opened the scoring. The Villa No.10 sent a delightful ball through the legs of Damon Park before drilling beyond a helpless Underwood. The Cottagers responded strongly and Kristian Sekularac found the side-netting before seeing his free-kick deflected over, while Park headed wide at the far post when well placed. Yet it was the home side who doubled their advantage midway through the first half, capitalising on a defensive mistake to feed Jimoh-Aloba, who skipped beyond Eddy Nsasi before applying a smart finish. Manager Gombau was forced into a change just after the half-hour mark as Kadan Young was replaced by Travis Patterson. And, amid driving rain, Fulham levelled courtesy of two quickfire strikes - Loupalo-Bi slotting past Sam Lewis before turning provider with a neat backheel for Works to equalise. Yet the Young Lions showed determination following the double setback and regained the lead immediately from kick-off, Patterson crossing for Burrowes to convert from close range. Villa stopper Lewis produced a diving save to deny Loupalo-Bi early in the second period as the tempo showed no sign of slowing. The Cottagers were applying plenty of pressure in search of a leveller and Lewis got down quickly to turn behind an effort from Sekularac, while Kane Taylor blocked on the line from substitute Imani Lanquedoc. Jimoh-Aloba came close to his hat-trick, firing over from 20 yards as the encounter entered the latter stages. Villa were then indebted to Lewis as he produced a fantastic block to crucially thwart Chris Donnell when clean through in the 84th minute. And it was the Young Lions who missed chances to add to the scoreline late on, sub Charlie Lutz going close before Jimoh-Aloba put narrowly wide with the goal at his mercy. And Villa were made to pay the price in injury time as De Jesus arrived at the back post amid a crowded penalty area to convert a low cross from close range, ensuring the hosts were forced to settle for a point apiece.
